function.name: generate_game_concept
function.description: Create or refine the high-level game concept, including core loop, genre, target playtime, unique hook, and target audience. Output a structured summary.

core_loop.description: One-sentence description of the main repeated gameplay

unique_hook.description: What makes this game stand out

target_playtime_minutes.description: Estimated total playtime for a full experience

target_audience.description: e.g. casual mobile players, hardcore PC gamers

function.name: create_feature_list
function.description: Break the game concept into must-have features, nice-to-have features, and out-of-scope items. Prioritize by MVP (minimum viable product) for small indie scope. Output as prioritized lists.

mvp_features.description: Core features needed for a playable prototype (keep to 5-8 max)

stretch_features.description: Polish or expansion ideas if time allows

out_of_scope.description: Features explicitly excluded to keep scope small

priority_order.description: Recommended implementation order for MVP features

function.name: define_milestones
function.description: Divide the project into 3–5 realistic milestones with clear deliverables, estimated effort (in dev days or sessions), and success criteria. Ideal for solo/small-team indie timelines (2–12 weeks).

function.name: assign_tasks_to_roles
function.description: Convert high-level features or milestone deliverables into specific, actionable tasks assigned to roles (Coder, Artist, Reviewer, etc.). Include acceptance criteria and dependencies.

dependencies.description: Previous task IDs or descriptions this depends on

function.name: balance_and_scope_check
function.description: Review proposed features/milestones for scope creep, fun factor, technical risk, and balance. Suggest cuts, simplifications, or tweaks to keep the game fun and finishable by a small team.
